records.ety: [Pref. in- not + conceivable: cf. F. inconcevable.]

defs: Not conceivable; incapable of being conceived by the mind; not explicable by the human intellect, or by any known principles or agencies; incomprehensible; as, it is inconceivable to us how the will acts in producing muscular motion.
quotes: It is inconceivable to me that a spiritual substance should represent an extended figure.
The inconceivableness of a quality existing without any subject to possess it.
